HCS: Hedera Consensus Service — timestamps events on the Hedera network in 3-5 seconds for ~$0.0001 per entry.
x402: An HTTP payment protocol that lets you pay per API call via crypto micropayments — no subscription needed.
escrow: HBAR held in a per-monitor account to pay for scheduled checks. You can withdraw remaining funds anytime by deleting the monitor.
proof chain: A cryptographically verifiable trail: detection → timestamp → storage → action. Each link is immutable.
Grove: A decentralized storage network (IPFS-compatible) used to store proof packages — screenshots, diffs, and evidence.
TinyFish: An AI-powered web intelligence engine that scans URLs for conditions you describe in plain English.
HBAR staking: Depositing HBAR into a monitor's escrow to fund automated background checks. ~0.5 ℏ debited per check.
ℏ: Hbar — the native cryptocurrency of the Hedera network. Used for payments and staking in LENITNES.
